description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single build stage that builds and pushes Docker images for multiple architectures using the docker:latest image and the docker:dind service. Define four jobs—docker-arm32v7-build, docker-arm64v8-build, docker-latest-build, and docker-nvidia-build—each running in the build stage, logging into the GitLab Container Registry with docker login, computing a branch‑specific tag and an architecture suffix, building the image with docker build --pull --build-arg BASE_IMAGE=... -t $IMAGE_NAME -f Dockerfile ., and pushing it with docker push $IMAGE_NAME. Each job sets its own BASE_IMAGE variable (node:20-bullseye-slim, arm64v8/node, node:22-bookworm-slim, nvidia/cuda:11.7.1-cudnn8-runtime-ubuntu22.04) and uses rules to run only on branches other than master, main, or dev when a Dockerfile exists. No caches or artifacts are defined. The pipeline publishes the built images to the GitLab Container Registry.
